blocks:
Ayah

إِذَا جَآءَكَ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقُونَ قَالُوا۟ نَشْهَدُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُهُۥ وَٱللَّهُ يَشْهَدُ إِنَّ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ لَكَـٰذِبُونَ (۱)
When the hypocrites come to you they say, ‘We bear witness that you are indeed the apostle of Allah.’ Allah knows that you are indeed His Apostle, and Allah bears witness that the hypocrites are indeed liars. (1)

Key Points

"Nifaq" (hypocrisy) comes from "Nafaq," meaning underground tunnels used for camouflage or escape. Some animals, like the field mouse and the lizard, build nests with two holes. The hypocrite (munafiq) also sets a hidden and secret path for himself so that he can escape through it when danger arises. People are divided into four categories: A) A group that believes in their hearts and acts upon their duties; these are the true believers (mu'min). B) A group that believes in their hearts but are not people of action; these are the wicked (fasiq). C) A group that does not believe in their hearts but outwardly performs the actions of believers; these are the hypocrites (munafiq). D) A group that neither believes in their hearts nor acts upon their duties; these are the disbelievers (kafir). (1) Imam Ali عليه السلام says: A person's hypocrisy stems from an inner feeling of lowliness. «نفاق المرء ذلّ یجده فى نفسه» (2)

Characteristics of the Hypocrite in the Quran

  1. Lying: «انّ المنافقین لكاذبون» (3)
  2. Aimless and wandering: «لا الى هؤلاء و لا الى هؤلاء» (4) They are neither with this group nor with that group.
  3. Lack of deep understanding: «لكن المنافقین لا یفقهون» (5)
  4. Rigidity and inflexibility: «كانّهم خُشب مسنّدة» (6)
  5. Prayer without enthusiasm: «قاموا كُسالى » (7) They stand for prayer lazily.
  6. Harsh speech and ill-temper: «سلقوكم بالسنةٍ حداد» (8) They assail you with sharp and harsh tongues.
  7. False oaths: «اتّخذوا أیمانهم جُنّة» They made their oaths a shield for their conspiracies.
  8. Criticizing the infallible leader: «یلمزك فى الصدقات» (9) They criticize the distribution of Zakat and do not consider you just in its division.
  9. Weakening the fighters: «لاتنفروا فى الحَرّ» (10) They say: The weather is hot, do not go to the front.
  10. Establishing a center of conspiracy in the name of a mosque: «و الّذین اتّخذوا مسجداً ضرارا» (11) (Opposite the Prophet's Mosque) they build a mosque so that (with their evil conspiracies) they may harm the Muslims.
  11. Breaking covenants: «و منهم من عاهد اللّه لئن اتانا من فضله لنصدّقنّ و لنكوننّ من الصّالحین فلمّا اتاهم من فضله بخلوا» (12) Some of them made a covenant with Allah that if He gave them of His bounty, they would surely give charity and be among the righteous. But when Allah gave them of His bounty, they became stingy and did not pay the Zakat due on their wealth.
  12. Belittling Muslims: «أ نؤمن كما آمن السفهاء» Should we believe as the foolish have believed?
  13. Economic siege of Muslims: «لاتنفقوا على من عند رسول اللّه حتى ینفضّوا» Do not spend on those who are with the Messenger of Allah until they disperse from around him.
  14. Deception and trickery: «یخادعون اللّه و الّذین آمنوا و ما یخدعون الاّ انفسهم» (13) They seek to deceive Allah and those who believe, while they deceive none but themselves.
  15. Stinginess in spending: «لاینفقون الاّ و هم كارهون» (14) They do not spend except reluctantly.
  16. Enjoining evil and forbidding good: «یأمرون بالمنكر و ینهون عن المعروف» (15)
  17. Fleeing from Jihad: «فرح المخلّفون بمقعدهم خلاف رسول اللّه و كرهوا ان یجاهدوا» (16) Those who remained behind (from the Battle of Tabuk) rejoiced in their sitting [at home] contrary to the Messenger of Allah, and they disliked that they should strive in Jihad.
  18. Spreading rumors: «و اذا جاءهم امر من الامن او الخوف اذاعوا به» (17) Whenever news of security or fear comes to them, they spread it (before investigation or consideration of expediency).

Because the danger posed by hypocrites is great, their characteristics have been discussed in several Surahs of the Quran, and hardly any Surah revealed in Medina fails to refer to the hypocrites, either explicitly or implicitly. (18)

Hypocrisy is a practical lie, the concealment of disbelief (kufr) and the manifestation of faith (iman). Of course, not every concealment is bad, such as concealing poverty or concealing worship.

Hypocrites were of two groups: a group that declared faith after Islam gained power but did not believe internally, and another group who believed initially but gradually became engrossed in worldly matters and lost their faith, yet continued to pretend to believe. The Islamic Ummah has always suffered irreparable damage from both groups.

During the time of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, there was much talk about hypocrites, but after his demise, the issue was silenced. Here are a few questions: 1. Was the Prophet's presence the cause of hypocrisy in a group, and did everyone become true believers after his demise?! 2. Were the hypocrites confined to the minority who gathered around Imam Ali, such as Salman, Abu Dharr, Miqdad, and others?!! While no one has ever considered these individuals hypocrites, or did the hypocrites find their goals realized among the majority and thus fell silent?

Hypocrites are liars both in this world: «انّ المنافقین لكاذبون», and in the Hereafter they will swear oaths that Allah calls them liars for: «یوم یبعثهم اللّه جمیعاً فیحلفون له كما یحلفون لكم و یحسبون انّهم على شى ء ألا انّهم هم الكاذبون» (19)

The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: I do not fear the believer or the disbeliever, but I fear the hypocrite. (20) On the other hand, He said: I do not fear the poverty of my Ummah; I fear their lack of prudence (or mismanagement). (21) Yes, if cunning hypocrites join forces with imprudent ignorant people, the Battle of Siffin and the event of Karbala occur.

1) Tafsir Ithna Ashari. 2) Ghurar al-Hikam. 3) Al-Munafiqun, 1. 4) An-Nisa, 143. 5) Al-Munafiqun, 7. 6) Al-Munafiqun, 4. 7) An-Nisa, 142. 8) Al-Ahzab, 19. 9) At-Tawbah, 58. 10) At-Tawbah, 81. 11) At-Tawbah, 107. 12) At-Tawbah, 75 - 76. 13) Al-Baqarah, 9. 14) At-Tawbah, 54. 15) At-Tawbah, 67. 16) At-Tawbah, 81. 17) An-Nisa, 83. 18) Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Quran. 19) Al-Mujadilah, 18. 20) Nahj al-Balaghah, Letter 27. 21) Awali al-La'ali, Vol. 4, p. 39.

Messages

  1. In early Islam, there were hypocritical elements that infiltrated the decision-making center of society, and even the Prophet was exposed to their conspiracies. «اذا جاءك المنافقون»
  2. The hypocrite uses a word of truth for a false purpose. «قالوا نشهد انّك لرسول اللّه»
  3. Take the true slogans and words from the unworthy and use them yourselves. «قالوا نشهد انّك لرسول اللّه و اللّه یعلم انّك لرسوله»
  4. Smooth talk and flattery are the methods of hypocrites. «نشهد انّك لرسول اللّه»
  5. Hypocrites speak with emphasis and oaths so that the listener believes their words. «نشهد انّك لرسول اللّه»
  6. Respond to emphasis with emphasis. «نشهد انّك لرسول... واللّه یشهد انّ المنافقین لكاذبون»
  7. Do not be pleased with every declaration of devotion and claim of support. «نشهد انك لرسول... لكاذبون»
  8. Expose the hypocrites. «واللّه یشهد ان المنافقین لكاذبون»
  9. Speaking contrary to one's belief is a lie, even if the statement itself is true. (Allah considers the hypocrites' statement to the Prophet that "You are the Messenger of Allah" a lie, because they said it contrary to their belief, even though the Prophet was truly the Messenger of Allah). «قالوا نشهد... و اللّه یشهد انّ المنافقین لكاذبون»
  10. True faith is conviction of the heart, not verbal affirmation. «قالوا... انّ المنافقین لكاذبون»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Munafiqun

This Surah, like Surah Al-Jumu'ah, has eleven verses and was revealed in Medina. The description of the characteristics of the hypocrites is not exclusive to this Surah; their spiritual and behavioral traits are mentioned in many Surahs of the Quran, especially the Medinan Surahs. The most comprehensive Surah regarding the hypocrites is Surah At-Tawbah, which dedicates about one hundred verses to describing their characteristics. Its recitation is recommended in the Friday Prayer. Perhaps the reason for this emphasis is so that the Islamic Ummah remains constantly vigilant against the conspiracies of the hypocrites. In jurisprudential books like Jawahir al-Kalam, it is stated: If the Friday Imam starts another Surah after Surah Al-Fatihah, he may switch to Surah Al-Munafiqun, provided he has not reached the middle of the Surah he started, unless the Surah he started was Al-Jumu'ah or At-Tawhid.

Ayahs

ٱتَّخَذُوٓا۟ أَيْمَـٰنَهُمْ جُنَّةً فَصَدُّوا۟ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ سَآءَ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۲)
They make a shield of their oaths, and bar from the way of Allah. Evil indeed is what they used to do. (2)
ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ ءَامَنُوا۟ ثُمَّ كَفَرُوا۟ فَطُبِعَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ فَهُمْ لَا يَفْقَهُونَ (۳)
That is because they believed and then disbelieved, so their hearts were sealed. Hence, they do not understand. (3)

Key Points

Junnah means a shield that covers the body from the enemy's arrow. Majnun (madman) is said of someone whose intellect has been covered. Jinn refers to a being hidden from people's eyes, and janin (fetus) refers to a baby hidden in the mother's womb. And Jannah (Paradise/Garden) is a garden whose shade and tree branches cover its ground.

The Quran has repeatedly spoken about the false oaths of the hypocrites: They swear by Allah to you to satisfy you. «یحلفون باللّه لكم لیرضوكم»(22); They swear that they had no goal other than goodwill in building the Mosque of Dirar. «لَیحلفنّ ان اردنا الاّ الحُسنى»(23); They will swear to you by Allah that if they had been able to participate in the battle (of Tabuk), they would have participated with you. «و سیحلفون باللّه لو استطعنا لخرجنا معكم»(24)

Throughout the Quran, wherever the name of the hypocrites is mentioned, harsh expressions are used, including: «طبع اللّه على قلوبهم» Allah has set a seal upon their hearts;(25) «لایفقهون» They have no understanding;(26) «لایعلمون» They do not know;(27) «لكاذبون» They are liars;(28) «ما یشعرون» They have no perception;(29) «مفسدون» They are corruptors;(30) «فى طغیانهم یعمهون»(31) They wander blindly in their transgression; «ما كانوا مهتدین»(32) They are not guided; «لن یغفر اللّه لهم»(33) Allah will never forgive them.

Blocking the way of Allah is sometimes done by the hypocrites through oaths. «ایمانهم جنة فصّدوا...» And sometimes it is done by the disbelievers through funding. «ان الّذین كفروا ینفقون أموالهم لیصدوا عن سبیل اللّه»(34) Indeed, those who disbelieved spend their wealth to avert people from the way of Allah.

Hypocrites block the way of Allah by any means they can, such as: breaking the unity of Muslims by building the Mosque of Dirar, inflicting military damage under the pretext of hot weather, inflicting economic damage by forbidding aid to the Prophet's companions, inflicting fatal damage through the conspiracy to martyr Imam Hassan and Imam Jawad علیهما السلام via their wives.

The leader of the Islamic society must be more vigilant than anyone else regarding the conspiracies of the hypocrites. In this Surah, the Prophet himself is addressed, although all Muslims are responsible: «اذا جاءك، نشهد انّك، رأیتَهم، تعجبك اجسامهم، تسمع لقولهم، فاحذرهم، استغفرتَ لهم»

22) At-Tawbah, 62. 23) At-Tawbah, 107. 24) At-Tawbah, 42. 25) Muhammad, 16. 26) Al-Munafiqun, 3. 27) At-Tawbah, 93. 28) Al-Munafiqun, 1. 29) Al-Baqarah, 9. 30) Al-Baqarah, 12. 31) Al-Baqarah, 15. 32) Al-Baqarah, 16. 33) Al-Munafiqun, 6. 34) Al-Anfal, 36.

Messages

1- Misusing sacred things and instrumental use of values is the method of the hypocrites. «اتّخذوا ایمانهم جُنّة»

2- The cultural work of the hypocrites is the use of religion against religion. «اتّخذوا ایمانهم جُنّة... فصدّوا عن سبیل اللّه»

3- Hypocrites strive to deprive others of Divine guidance. «فصدّوا عن سبیل اللّه» (The goal of the hypocrites is to block the way of Allah, but which other path people take is not important.)

4- The actions of the hypocrites are entirely ugly and undesirable. «ساء ما كانوا یعملون»

5- The ultimate outcome of hypocrisy is disbelief (kufr). «ذلك بانّهم آمنوا ثم كفروا»

6- Through hypocrisy, a person sets a seal upon his own heart and deprives it of understanding the truths. «فطبع على قلوبهم فهم لایفقهون»

Ayah

۞ وَإِذَا رَأَيْتَهُمْ تُعْجِبُكَ أَجْسَامُهُمْ ۖ وَإِن يَقُولُوا۟ تَسْمَعْ لِقَوْلِهِمْ ۖ كَأَنَّهُمْ خُشُبٌ مُّسَنَّدَةٌ ۖ يَحْسَبُونَ كُلَّ صَيْحَةٍ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ هُمُ ٱلْعَدُوُّ فَٱحْذَرْهُمْ ۚ قَـٰتَلَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۖ أَنَّىٰ يُؤْفَكُونَ (٤)
When you see them, their bodies impress you, and if they speak, you listen to their speech. Yet they are like dry logs set reclining [against a wall]. They suppose every cry is directed against them. They are the enemy, so beware of them. May Allah assail them, where do they stray?! (4)

Key Points

In Surah At-Tawbah, Allah tells the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله not to be astonished by the wealth and children of the hypocrites. Here (in Surah Al-Munafiqun), He says that their appearance and speech should not cause you astonishment. Although the addressee in this Ayah is the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, the intent is all Muslims, who might be influenced by the appearances and speech of the hypocrites.

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: A believer has four enemies: a Muslim who envies him: «مؤمن یحسُدَه», a hypocrite who harbors hatred for him: «منافق یبغضه», a devil who misguides him: «شیطان یضله», and a disbeliever who fights him: «کافر یقاتله».(35)

Wood is useful if it is used in the ceiling, walls, doors, and windows, but if it is leaned against something in a corner, it is a sign of its uselessness. «خشبٌ مسنّدة» (propped-up timbers/pieces of wood). The comparison of the hypocrites to cut tree trunks, «كانّهم خشب مسنّدة» (as if they were propped-up timbers), can be due to several aspects, including: 1. Lightness, hollowness, and breaking under pressure and impact; 2. Rigidity, dryness, lack of flexibility, and inability to be influenced; 3. Lack of independence in standing on their own feet; 4. Lack of ability to hear and reflect.

The hypocrites are apprehensive of the believers and are constantly worried that an Ayah might be revealed concerning them: «یحسبون كلّ صیحة علیهم» (They think every cry is against them), similar to Ayah 64 of At-Tawbah, which states: «یحذر المنافقون ان تنزل علیهم سورة تنبّئهم بما فى قلوبهم» (The hypocrites are apprehensive lest a Surah should be revealed about them, informing them of what is in their hearts).

«هم العدوّ» (They are the enemy) means that these individuals are the real enemy, because, firstly, they are inside the community and are aware of the Muslims' secrets; secondly, because they are in the guise of a friend, recognizing them is difficult; thirdly, because they are unknown, fighting them is harder; fourthly, because they have familial and marital ties with Muslims, dealing with them is more complicated; and fifthly, their blow is more surprising (or unexpected).

35) Nahj al-Fasahah, p. 481.

Messages

1- The hypocrites are among us and right before our eyes, not people hidden from view. «رأیتهم»

2- We should not trust every appearance, as an ascetic look is not proof of piety. In some cases, a beautiful appearance is a means of deceiving people. «تعجبك اجسامهم»

3- If it were not for the Divine warnings and the Prophet's attention, beautiful and deceptive words would compel even the Prophet to listen. «و ان یقولوا تسمع لقولهم»

4- Hypocrites have a calm exterior but a troubled interior, and they are always in fear and anxiety. «تعجبك اجسامهم... یحسبون كلّ صیحة علیهم»

5- Fear the internal enemy more. «هم العدو»

6- We must recognize the enemy and not be deceived by his appearance or his words. «تعجبك اجسامهم... تسمع لقولهم... هم العدوّ فاحذرهم»

7- The hypocrites are in conflict with Allah, and Allah curses them: «قاتَلهم اللّه». (The verb «قاتل» is from the mufā‘alah form, indicating mutual conflict, which is why He did not say: «قتلهم اللّه».)

8- The stance of the hypocrites against Islam is astonishing. «اَنّى تُوفكون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَعَالَوْا۟ يَسْتَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ رَسُولُ ٱللَّهِ لَوَّوْا۟ رُءُوسَهُمْ وَرَأَيْتَهُمْ يَصُدُّونَ وَهُم مُّسْتَكْبِرُونَ (٥)
When they are told, ‘Come, that Allah’s Apostle may plead for forgiveness for you,’ they twist their heads, and you see them turn away disdainfully. (5)

Ayah

سَوَآءٌ عَلَيْهِمْ أَسْتَغْفَرْتَ لَهُمْ أَمْ لَمْ تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ لَن يَغْفِرَ ٱللَّهُ لَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلْفَـٰسِقِينَ (٦)
It is the same for them whether you plead for forgiveness for them, or do not plead for forgiveness for them: Allah will never forgive them. Indeed Allah does not guide the transgressing lot. (6)

Key Points

The word Ta'ālaw (تعالوا) originates from ‘uluww (علوّ), meaning an invitation to come towards elevation and growth. Lawwaw (لَوّوا), from the root layy (لىّ), means the twisting of a rope, and is used in the context of turning away from the truth. The greatest misfortune is when a person both deprives himself: Lawwaw ru'ūsahum (لوّوا رؤسهم), and deprives others: Yaṣuddūn (یصدّون); especially if these actions are conscious. Wa hum mustakbirūn (و هم مستكبرون). A verse similar to verse 6 is also found in verse 80 of Sūrah At-Tawbah: «استغفر لهم او لا تستغفر لهم ان تستغفر لهم سبعین مرّة فلن یغفر اللّه لهم» If you seek forgiveness for the hypocrites who sting the believers seventy times, (as long as they do not abandon their hypocrisy) Allah will never forgive them.

Messages

1- We should approach misguided individuals and invite them to repentance and rehabilitation. Wa idhā qīla lahum ta‘ālaw yastaghfir lakum... (و اذا قیل لهم تعالوا یستغفر لكم...)

2- Responding to the invitation of the Divine Saints (Awliyā') leads to elevation and growth. Ta‘ālaw (تعالوا).

3- The prayer of God's Saints for the forgiveness of sinners is answered. Yastaghfir lakum (یستغفر لكم).

4- Seeking intercession (Tawassul) through the Prophet of Allah is permissible. Ta‘ālaw yastaghfir lakum Rasūlullāh (تعالوا یستغفر لكم رسول اللّه).

5- Refusing a beneficial proposal is a sign of arrogance (Takabbur). Lawwaw ru'ūsahum... wa hum mustakbirūn (لوّوا رؤسهم... و هم مستكبرون).

6- For one who does not accept the invitation to truth, even the prayer of the Prophet concerning him is ineffective. Ta‘ālaw... lawwaw ru'ūsahum... sawā'un ‘alayhim istaghfarta lahum... (تعالوا... لوّوا رؤوسهم... سواء علیهم استغفرت لهم...).

7- Arrogance (Takabbur) and pride (Istikbār) deprive a person of the boundless Divine Mercy. Hum mustakbirūn... lan yaghfira Allāhu lahum (هم مستكبرون... لن یغفر اللّه لهم).

8- Guidance and misguidance are in the hand of Allah, but He deprives of guidance only those who pursue transgression and sin. Inna Allāha lā yahdī al-qawma al-fāsiqīn (انّ اللّه لا یهدى القوم الفاسقین).

Ayah

هُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ لَا تُنفِقُوا۟ عَلَىٰ مَنْ عِندَ رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ حَتَّىٰ يَنفَضُّوا۟ ۗ وَلِلَّهِ خَزَآئِنُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ لَا يَفْقَهُونَ (۷)
They are the ones who say, ‘Do not spend on those who are with the Apostle of Allah until they scatter off [from around him].’ Yet to Allah belong the treasuries of the heavens and the earth, but the hypocrites do not understand. (7)

Key Points

Khazā'in (خزائن) is the plural of Khazīnah (خزینة), meaning treasure or treasury. One of the methods used by the enemy is economic siege and putting believers in distress. Just as the hypocrites used to say to one another: Do not spend on the companions of the Prophet so that they disperse from around him. Mu'awiyah also issued a directive ordering that the companions of Ali عليه السلام be deprived of the public treasury (Bayt al-Mal). «انظروا من قامت علیه البیّنة انّه یحبّ علیّاً و اهل بیته فامحوه من الدّیوان و اسقطوا عطائه و رزقه»(36) Just as they also cut off water from the companions of Imam Husayn in Karbala, today superpowers use the pressure of economic siege to force nations into submission.

36) Sharh Nahj al-Balaghah, Ibn Abi al-Hadid, Vol. 11, p. 44.

Messages

1- One of the enemy's ways to break the school of thought (Maktab) and the leader is to scatter the followers. «لاتنفقوا... حتى ینفضّوا»

2- Materialistic individuals have a materialistic analysis and think that people have gathered around the Prophet for worldly gain; therefore, they say: Do not spend so that they disperse. «لاتنفقوا... حتى ینفضّوا...»

3- Believers should not look hopefully toward the aid of hypocrites and opponents during times of distress; rather, their reliance (Tawakkul) must be upon Allah and His treasures. «لاتنفقوا... للّه خزائن السّموات و الارض»

4- Give hope to your own people and respond to the enemy's vain talk. «لاتنفقوا... وللّه خزائن...»

5- The understanding of the hypocrites is based on superficial calculations and lacks depth. «لكنّ المنافقین لایفقهون»

Ayah

يَقُولُونَ لَئِن رَّجَعْنَآ إِلَى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ لَيُخْرِجَنَّ ٱلْأَعَزُّ مِنْهَا ٱلْأَذَلَّ ۚ وَلِلَّهِ ٱلْعِزَّةُ وَلِرَسُولِهِۦ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۸)
They say, ‘When we return to the city, the mighty will surely expel the weak from it.’ Yet all might belongs to Allah and His Apostle and the faithful, but the hypocrites do not know. (8)

Key Points

In the exegeses (Tafasir), a detailed occasion of revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) is mentioned for Ayahs 5 through 8 of Surah Al-Munafiqun, which we will summarize here: After the Battle of Banu Al-Mustaliq, which occurred in the sixth year of the Hijra, during the return to Medina, a dispute arose between two Muslims—one from the Muhajirun (Emigrants) and the other from the Ansar (Helpers)—over drawing water from a well. Each sought help from his friends to overpower the other. Abdullah ibn Ubayy, who was the leader of the hypocrites (Munafiqun), rushed to the aid of the Ansari man and said: We gave the Muhajirun shelter and wealth, but now they stand against us. If we return to Medina, the honored ones (the 'A'azz) will surely expel the disgraced ones (the 'Adhall).

Zayd ibn Arqam, who was a young man, reported this statement to the Prophet. The Prophet summoned Abdullah ibn Ubayy, but he denied it. The Ansar said: Why should our elder, Abdullah, be reprimanded because of the report of a young boy? The Prophet ordered the caravan to move and did not allow a rest stop, so that the people would not have the opportunity or leisure to continue their discussions. When they reached Medina, the son of Abdullah ibn Ubayy, who was a true believer, blocked his father's path and said: Are you the most honored person in Medina, and do you want to expel the Prophet? I will never let you enter unless the Noble Prophet grants permission. The matter was reported to the Prophet, and He granted him permission to enter Medina. Abdullah, who considered himself honored, became disgraced (dhaleel) and remained in his house out of intense shame until he died. Whenever he was told, "Come, let us go to the Prophet and apologize," he refused.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Allah has entrusted the affairs of the Muslims to themselves, but He has not permitted them to disgrace themselves: «و لم یفوّض الیه ان یكون ذلیلاً» (37). He then recited the Ayah: «وللّه العزّة و لرسوله و للمؤمنین».

We read in a Hadith that they asked: How does a believer disgrace himself? Imam Sadiq عليه السلام replied: «یتعرّض لما لایطیق» (He exposes himself to what he cannot bear) and «یدخل فیما یعتذر منه» (He enters into that for which he must apologize) (38). (Meaning: He engages in a task that he is unable to perform or for which he must later make excuses.)

In the phrase «وللّه العزّة» (And to Allah belongs the honor), because li-Llah (to Allah) precedes al-'Izzah (the honor), exclusivity is inferred. That is, true honor belongs exclusively to Allah. The honor of Allah is intrinsic to His Essence, the honor of the Prophet is due to the special favors granted to him by Allah, and the honor of the believers is due to the Divine promise of their ultimate victory and triumph (39).

37) Kafi, Vol. 5, p. 63, quoted from Tafsir Rahnama. 38) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 39) Tafsir Atyab al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The hypocrites seek to overthrow the Islamic government. «لیخرجنّ الاعزّ منها الاذلّ»

2- The hypocrites consider themselves honored and the Prophet and the believers disgraced. «لیخرجنّ الاعزّ منها الاذلّ»

3- Allah has guaranteed the victory and honor of the believers, provided they remain believers. «وللّه العزة و لرسوله و...»

4- Honor is exclusive to Allah, the Prophet, and the believers. «وللّه العزّة و لرسوله و للمؤمنین»

5- Respond to the enemy's nonsense and tell those who imagine themselves to be the most honored people and the Prophet the most disgraced: «وللّه العزّة و لرسوله و للمؤمنین»

6- The hypocrites are incapable of recognizing the truth. They have neither the power to comprehend that Allah provides sustenance to the believers despite economic sanctions: «لایفقهون» (They do not understand), nor that true honor belongs to the people of faith. «لایعلمون» (They do not know).

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تُلْهِكُمْ أَمْوَٰلُكُمْ وَلَآ أَوْلَـٰدُكُمْ عَن ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَمَن يَفْعَلْ ذَٰلِكَ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ (۹)
O you who have faith! Do not let your possessions and children distract you from the remembrance of Allah, and whoever does that—it is they who are the losers. (9)

Key Points

Up to this point, the verses related to the hypocrites have concluded. However, since one of the causes of hypocrisy is excessive attachment to the world, this verse warns the believers that wealth and children should not distract you (40).

Distraction from the remembrance of Allah: The factors that prevent the remembrance of Allah are numerous, but the most important of them are wealth and children, which this verse points to. «لاتلهكم اموالكم و اولادكم عن ذكر اللّه» Wine and gambling are also barriers to the remembrance of Allah. «یصدّكم عن ذكر اللّه» (41) Trade and commerce can also be obstacles. Therefore, the Quran praises those whose trade and commerce have not diverted them from the remembrance of Allah. «رجال لاتلهیهم تجارة و لابیع عن ذكر اللّه» (42) Accumulation and excessive seeking (of increase) are deterrent factors. «الهاكم التكاثر» (43) Long hopes are also a barrier to the remembrance of Allah. «یلههم الامل» (44) Excessive comfort (affluence) is another factor leading to heedlessness. «متّعتهم و آبائهم حتى نسوا الذّكر» (45)

Of course, attachment to the world is dangerous when a person does not view the world as a prelude to the Hereafter, and his goal in all matters is the world. «فاعرض عن من تولّى عن ذكرنا و لم یرد الاّ الحیاة الدنیا» (46) Turn away from him who turns away from Our remembrance and desires nothing but the life of this world.

It is clear that heedlessness regarding the remembrance of Allah leads to companionship with Satan: «و من یعشُ عن ذكر الرّحمن نقیض له شیطانا فهو له قرین» (47) and subjects man to a severe and escalating punishment. «و من یُعرض عن ذكر ربّه یسلكه عذاباً صَعَداً» (48)

What is dangerous is heedlessness regarding the remembrance of Allah; mentioning wealth and children is because these two are the strongest factors leading to heedlessness. The phrase «ذكر اللّه» (remembrance of Allah) includes all forms of remembering God, but the most prominent remembrance of God is Salat (prayer), and one must be careful that attending to wealth and children does not prevent one from prayer. Perhaps the reason wealth and children act as barriers is their outward allurements, which make man heedless of God. «المال و البنون زینة الحیاة الدنیا» (49) The Quran considers wealth and children a source of fitnah (trial): «واعلموا انّما اموالكم و اولادكم فتنة» (50) and states: «و ما اموالكم و لا اولادكم بالّتى تقرّبكم عندنا زلفى» (51) Wealth and children are not the means of your proximity to Allah.

40) Tafsir-e Nemooneh. 41) Al-Ma'idah, 91. 42) An-Nur, 37. 43) At-Takathur, 1. 44) Al-Hijr, 3. 45) Al-Furqan, 18. 46) An-Najm, 29. 47) Az-Zukhruf, 36. 48) Al-Jinn, 17. 49) Al-Kahf, 46. 50) Al-Anfal, 28. 51) Saba', 37.

Messages

1- In order to admonish the believers and ensure they accept the advice, grant them dignity (by addressing them respectfully). «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا...» (O you who have believed...)

2- The requirement of faith is prioritizing the remembrance of Allah over wealth and children. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا لاتلهكم اموالكم...»

3- Wealth and children, whether little or much, can be a barrier to the remembrance of Allah. «اموالكم... اولادكم» (Your wealth... your children)

4- The real loss is heedlessness regarding the remembrance of Allah. «فاولئك هم الخاسرون» (And those are the losers.) (Losing wealth and children is a partial loss, but heedlessness regarding the Creator is a total loss.)

Ayah

وَأَنفِقُوا۟ مِن مَّا رَزَقْنَـٰكُم مِّن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِىَ أَحَدَكُمُ ٱلْمَوْتُ فَيَقُولَ رَبِّ لَوْلَآ أَخَّرْتَنِىٓ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ فَأَصَّدَّقَ وَأَكُن مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۱٠)
Spend out of what We have provided you before death comes to any of you, whereat he might say, ‘My Lord, why did You not respite me for a short time so that I could give charity and become one of the righteous!’ (10)

Ayah

وَلَن يُؤَخِّرَ ٱللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِذَا جَآءَ أَجَلُهَا ۚ وَٱللَّهُ خَبِيرٌۢ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ (۱۱)
But Allah will never respite anyone when his time has come, and Allah is well aware of what you do. (11)

Key Points

| In Ayah 7, the hypocrites were saying: Do not help the companions of the Prophet so that they disperse from around him. Perhaps this Ayah, in order to neutralize their plan, tells the believers: Since you have faith, help the believers so that the area around the Prophet does not become empty. If a person believes in death and the Day of Judgment, he will spend very easily. The Quran has raised the issue of the Resurrection and the Day of Judgment several times to motivate spending (Infaq), including in Surah Al-Baqarah, Ayah 254, where it says: «انفقوا مما رزقناكم من قبل ان یأتى یوم لا بیع فیه ولا خلّة ولا شفاعة» Spend from what We have provided you before the Day comes when there will be no trade, no friendship, and no intercession. Ibn Abbas says: This request for opportunity at the moment of death is for those who have not paid the Zakat on their wealth or were capable (mustati‘) but did not perform Hajj.(52) The request for respite and return to the world is not accepted, neither at the threshold of death, as Ayah 100 of Surah Al-Mu'minun and this Ayah state, nor on the Day of Judgment, when the inhabitants of Hell will say: «ربنا اخرجنا منها فان عدنا فانا ظالمون»(53) Imam Baqir عليه السلام, citing the Ayah «و لن یُوخر اللّه نفساً», said: Every year on the Night of Qadr (Laylat al-Qadr), all affairs until the next Night of Qadr are recorded, finalized, and revealed, and no change or delay occurs in them.(54)

52) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 53) Al-Mu'minun, 107. 54) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Prepare the ground for inviting people to good deeds. «انفقوا... من قبل أن یأتى احدكم الموت» (Remind them of the arrival of death, the lack of opportunity for return, and the plea for delaying death).

2- What we possess is from Allah, not from ourselves. «رزقناكم»

3- The remedy for an event must be sought before its occurrence. «من قبل ان یأتى» (With the arrival of death, the path of action is closed).

4- Death arrives suddenly and cannot be delayed. «یأتى احدكم الموت... لولا اخّرتنى»

5- The wealthy who were not charitable during their lifetime will feel great regret at the time of death. «ربّ لولا اخرتنى... فاصّدّق»

6- The sign of worthiness and righteousness is spending (infaq) on the deprived. «اكن من الصّالحین»
